title: Sets & Venn Diagrams
learning_objectives: Use union, intersection, and complement correctly.
Apply the two-set formula |A∪B| = |A| + |B| − |A∩B| and account for 'neither'.
Translate overlap word problems into a Venn structure (only-A, only-B, both, neither).
Find 'only one group' counts from totals and overlaps.

pedagogical_model.sequence: I do → we do → you do

items.prompt: For the same sets, list A ∪ B.
How many elements are in that A ∪ B?
Of 30 students, 18 play a sport. How many do not?
If 18 like tea and 15 like coffee in a class of 30, why isn't the total 33?
items.answer: People who like BOTH are counted twice; the overlap is double-counted.

retrieval_starter.purpose: Retrieves union/intersection/complement and exposes the double-counting problem that inclusion–exclusion fixes.
retrieval_starter.if_students_struggle: If union vs intersection blurs, anchor: 'or' = union (everything), 'and' = intersection (overlap only).

questions.prompt: Of 30 students, 18 play a sport. How many do not?

opening.prior_knowledge_bridge: Q5 names today's whole idea: when you add two group sizes, the people in both get counted twice. The fix is to subtract the overlap once — that's the two-set formula. Combined with 'neither', it turns any overlap word problem into a quick arithmetic setup.
opening.learning_intention: By the end you can solve two-set overlap problems with |A∪B| = |A| + |B| − |A∩B|, include a 'neither' group, and extract 'only-A' counts.
success_criteria: I can find the overlap when given two group sizes and the union (or total and neither).
I can find how many are in exactly one group.
I can lay out only-A / only-B / both / neither and have them sum to the total.

opening_script.move: Check; dramatize the double-count in Q5.
Bridge: subtract the overlap once → the formula.
I do: languages with a 'neither' group.

i_do.stem: In a group of 40 people, 25 speak French, 18 speak Spanish, and 7 speak both languages. How many speak neither?
i_do.explanation: Speakers of at least one = 25 + 18 − 7 = 36. Neither = total − union = 40 − 36 = 4.
think_aloud_steps: First find how many speak at least one language (the union).
i_do.key_takeaway: Total = only-A + only-B + both + neither; equivalently Total = |A| + |B| − |both| + neither.

we_do.stem: In a class of 30, 20 study math and 14 study physics; 8 study both. How many study at least one of the two?
scaffold_hints: Add the two groups, then remove the overlap once.

immediate_feedback.if_correct: Right — subtracting the overlap once avoids double counting.
immediate_feedback.if_incorrect: 34 double-counts the 8 who do both; subtract them once.

we_do.stem: Of 50 people, 30 own a car, 25 own a bike, and 10 own neither. How many own both?
we_do.explanation: Own at least one = 50 − 10 = 40. Then 40 = 30 + 25 − both, so both = 55 − 40 = 15.
scaffold_hints: Find 'at least one' first: total − neither = 50 − 10 = 40.

immediate_feedback.if_incorrect: Subtract 'neither' from the total to get the union, then solve for the overlap.

you_do.stem: In a survey of 60 people, 35 like apples and 40 like bananas. Every person likes at least one. How many like both?
you_do.explanation: Union = 60 (everyone likes at least one), so both = 35 + 40 − 60 = 15.

you_do.stem: Set A has 12 elements, set B has 18 elements, and A ∩ B has 5 elements. How many elements are in A ∪ B?
In a class of 36, every student takes art, music, or both. 22 take art and 20 take music. How many take ONLY art?
Of 100 people, 60 drink coffee, 50 drink tea, and 30 drink both. How many drink coffee but NOT tea?
you_do.explanation: Coffee but not tea = |coffee| − |both| = 60 − 30 = 30. (The union is 80 and 20 drink neither — consistent.)

sample_held_out_items.stem: A = {2, 4, 6, 8} and B = {4, 8, 12}. How many elements are in A ∪ B?
Of 80 people, 45 read fiction, 38 read nonfiction, and 12 read neither. How many read both?

mastery_check.definition: A topic is mastered at >=85% correct on new/held-out-style questions across >=2 spaced sessions with delay (PRD Section 9.2). Flashcard recall alone is not mastery; application under mixed, delayed conditions is the bar.
conditions: >=85% correct on new/held-out-style items (not flashcard recall) per session
sustained across >=2 spaced sessions with a delay between them
per-topic memory surfaced only after >=20 graded reviews (PRD give-up rule)
per-topic performance surfaced only after >=8 application attempts (PRD give-up rule)
mastery_check.on_fail: Not yet: route every miss to the required error log (tag why: content gap / reasoning flaw / timing / careless / trap), schedule spaced re-practice via topic-aware scheduling, then re-enter the we-do -> you-do loop before re-testing.

citations.name: OpenStax, Introductory Statistics — Venn diagrams & set operations
citations.note: Unions, intersections, and complements in the probability chapter.

citations.name: GMAC — GMAT Focus Edition (official exam overview)
citations.note: Official exam structure and Quantitative Reasoning scope.
